opening_complaint.verbatim_patient_text: My stomach feels completely paralysed — I'm throwing up everything I try, even water, and the room spins violently the moment I try to stand up. I can't keep anything down and I feel awful.
opening_complaint.stated_reason_for_presenting_today: It's been about a day now, I've been sick over and over into a basin for the last 12 hours, and I can't stand up without nearly blacking out. I don't know what to do.

symptom_narrative.onset: started building yesterday, so over the past 24 hours, and the vomiting has been non-stop for about the last 12 hours
symptom_narrative.character: intense nausea, really bad bloating and my belly feels swollen and tight, waves of cramping stomach pain, and I keep vomiting
symptom_narrative.severity_patient_description: the nausea and dizziness are the worst part — I'd say the pain is a 7 out of 10 in waves, and I feel completely wiped out
symptom_narrative.location_as_patient_describes: all over my stomach, it feels swollen and tight everywhere rather than one spot
symptom_narrative.timing_pattern: constant, with waves of cramping; the dizziness is worst when I stand or sit up
symptom_narrative.what_makes_it_better: lying completely flat helps the dizziness a bit; nothing helps the nausea
symptom_narrative.what_makes_it_worse: standing up makes my heart pound and race and the room spins; trying to drink or eat makes me vomit straight away
symptom_narrative.associated_symptoms_volunteered: I feel really clammy and sweaty, my mouth is bone dry, and I've barely passed any urine today. I also can't seem to empty my bladder properly — it feels full but won't come out.
symptom_narrative.previous_episodes: I get flares like this because of my connective tissue condition, but this one is the worst I've had — usually I can still keep some fluids down
symptom_narrative.functional_impact: I'm stuck in bed, I can't stand up without nearly fainting, and I've been vomiting into a basin

past_medical_history.condition_as_patient_states: hypermobile Ehlers-Danlos syndrome (a connective tissue condition I've had diagnosed for years)
past_medical_history.how_managed: managed by my GP and specialists; I get flares affecting my gut and my blood pressure/heart rate

past_medical_history.condition_as_patient_states: problems with my automatic body functions — my heart races and I get very dizzy when I stand, and my digestion is often very slow
past_medical_history.how_managed: lifestyle measures, fluids and salt, and medications my specialists manage

past_medical_history.condition_as_patient_states: long-standing fatigue that I live with at baseline

current_medications_as_reported.name_as_patient_states: I take some regular medications and supplements my specialists manage for the flares

social_history_volunteered.relevant_life_context: lives alone; feeling frightened being this unwell on her own and unable to stand safely

objective_data_offered.value: haven't been able to keep any water down for about 12 hours and have barely passed any urine today
when I stand up my heart pounds and races and the room spins so badly I nearly black out
objective_data_offered.reliability_caveat: patient-reported sensation, no home blood-pressure cuff or heart-rate device used

objective_data_offered.value: looks pale and sweaty on camera, lips and mouth look dry, lying flat and appears exhausted
has been vomiting repeatedly into a basin; the vomit is not green/bile-coloured and there is no blood in it
